raw_text:
VectorShift AI // Manager, MLOps Engineering

San Francisco, CA (Remote-Friendly) | Full-time | $210,000 - $270,000 base + equity + bonus

Production-grade machine learning infrastructure for enterprises at scale

>> ABOUT

VectorShift AI is a Series B machine learning infrastructure company that helps enterprise
data teams build, deploy, and monitor production ML systems. We provide integrated MLOps

platforms that streamline the entire ML lifecycle, from experimentation to model serving
and monitoring. Our customers include leading financial services, healthcare, and e-

commerce firms managing thousands of ML models in production.

>> SUMMARY

We are hiring a Manager of MLOps Engineering to lead our growing platform engineering team.

You will oversee a team of 5-7 MLOps engineers, drive architectural decisions for our model
orchestration and monitoring platform, and collaborate with product and customer success to

deliver world-class ML infrastructure. This is a high-impact role requiring both hands-on
technical depth and proven people management experience. You should be comfortable with

Kubernetes, model serving, and ML workflow orchestration while mentoring engineers and
setting technical vision.

>> RESPONSIBILITIES

- Lead and mentor a team of 5-7 MLOps engineers, conducting 1:1s, performance reviews,
and professional development planning

- Design and oversee the architecture of MLOps infrastructure supporting model training,
deployment, and monitoring at scale using Kubeflow, Airflow, and Kubernetes

- Establish best practices and operational standards for ML workflow orchestration, model
registry management via MLflow, and CI/CD integration

- Own technical roadmap for the MLOps platform, balancing customer needs with engineering
feasibility and long-term scalability

- Drive cross-functional collaboration between engineering, product, and customer success
teams to align on MLOps priorities and execution

- Implement comprehensive model monitoring and observability solutions, including data
drift detection and model performance tracking

- Recruit, interview, and onboard new MLOps engineers to expand team capacity and
diversity of thought

- Lead incident response and post-mortems for production issues affecting customer ML
systems, fostering a culture of reliability

- Represent engineering in customer meetings and technical design reviews, communicating
complex architectural trade-offs effectively

>> MUST HAVE

- 6+ years of experience in MLOps, platform engineering, or related infrastructure roles,
with at least 2 years in a management or senior technical leadership capacity

- Demonstrated proficiency with Kubernetes, Docker, and container orchestration platforms
in production environments

 - Strong hands-on experience with ML workflow orchestration tools (Airflow, Kubeflow, or

equivalent) and model lifecycle management (MLflow or similar)
- Proven track record of building and scaling technical teams, including hiring,

mentorship, and performance management
- Deep understanding of ML deployment patterns, model serving frameworks, and MLOps best

practices
- Experience with monitoring, observability, and alerting systems for ML systems (e.g.,

Prometheus, ELK, custom ML metrics)
- Strong communication skills with ability to influence and align stakeholders across

engineering, product, and customer-facing teams

>> NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with feature stores (Feast, Tecton, or comparable platforms) and their role

in ML systems
- Familiarity with model serving platforms (KServe, Seldon, BentoML) and online/batch

prediction architectures
- Background in distributed systems, cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, Azure), and

infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Helm)
- Prior experience scaling technical organizations or managing through periods of high

growth
- Contribution to open-source ML or infrastructure projects

>> STACK

Kubernetes & Docker / Apache Airflow / Kubeflow / MLflow / Prometheus & Grafana / Feature Stores

(Feast/Tecton) / KServe / Python / Jenkins/GitHub Actions / PostgreSQL/DynamoDB

>> BENEFITS

- Competitive salary and equity package with standard 4-year vesting
- Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% company contribution

for employees
- Unlimited PTO with minimum 20 days encouraged, plus 10 company holidays

- Professional development budget ($5k/year) for conferences, courses, and certifications
- Parental leave (16 weeks paid for primary caregiver, 8 weeks for secondary) and

fertility support
- Remote-friendly work environment with flexible hours; optional office in San Francisco

- Monthly lunch stipend and regular team offsites/retreats
- Mental health support including therapy stipend and meditation app subscriptions

>> APPLY

Apply via our careers page at vectorshift.ai/careers with your resume and a cover letter
highlighting your MLOps experience and team leadership background. Shortlisted candidates

will have an initial screening call with our Talent team, followed by technical and
leadership-focused interviews with the VP of Engineering and existing team members. We

review applications on a rolling basis and aim to move quickly with strong candidates.
